CAN: project any GL(3,R) element to its O(3) factor via QR decomposition
verify polar decomposition A = Q*S (Q ∈ O(3), S symmetric pos-def)
prove non-commutativity of GL(3,R) and O(3) elements (ratchet direction)
exclude GL(3,R)\O(3) elements from O(3) via z3 UNSAT
decompose gl(3) = so(3) ⊕ sym(3) via sympy

pytorch.reason: load-bearing: pytorch polar decomposition (QR) maps any GL(3,R) element to its O(3) factor; the metric constraint is imposed by extracting Q.

pyg.reason: not used in this pairwise GL(3)↔O(3) coupling probe; cross-tool coupling beyond the two target shells is deferred.

z3.reason: load-bearing: z3 UNSAT proves no element can be in GL(3,R)\O(3) (i.e., det≠0 AND M^TM≠I) while simultaneously satisfying M^TM=I.

cvc5.reason: not used in this pairwise GL(3)↔O(3) coupling probe; cross-tool coupling beyond the two target shells is deferred.

sympy.reason: load-bearing: sympy decomposes gl(3) = so(3) + sym(3) into antisymmetric (so(3)) and symmetric parts; the metric constraint projects onto so(3) = Lie(O(3)).

clifford.reason: load-bearing: in Cl(3,0), O(3) acts via versors (grade-1 elements); GL(3,R)\O(3) elements distort the metric but remain grade-1 in the algebra.

geomstats.reason: load-bearing: geomstats SpecialOrthogonal confirms identity belongs to SO(3); GL(3)→O(3) coupling is validated through the manifold structure.

rustworkx.reason: load-bearing: rustworkx encodes the GL(3)→O(3) coupling as the first directed edge in the G-tower DAG; verified via adjacency query.

A_in_GL3.detail: A has det≠0: confirmed in GL(3,R)

projection_to_O3.detail: QR decomposition extracts the O(3) factor from a GL(3,R) element

O3_element_in_GL3.detail: O(3) element (rotation) is in GL(3,R): O(3) ⊂ GL(3,R)

noncommutativity_GL3_O3.detail: A∘R ≠ R∘A for A ∈ GL(3,R)\O(3): non-commutativity defines ratchet direction

polar_decomposition_A_equals_QS.detail: Polar decomp A = Q * S: GL(3,R)/O(3) ≅ pos-definite symmetric matrices

z3_GL_minus_O_not_O.detail: z3 UNSAT: element in GL(1,R)\O(1) cannot simultaneously satisfy x^2=1

gl3_decomp_antisym_plus_sym.detail: gl(3) = so(3) + sym(3): antisymmetric + symmetric decomposition

projection_to_so3.detail: Antisymmetric part of gl(3) element ∈ so(3) = Lie(O(3))

clifford_O3_versor_unit.detail: O(3) versor (unit vector) has n^2 = 1 in Cl(3,0)

clifford_GL3_non_unit_versor.detail: Scaled vector (GL\O) has n^2 = 4 ≠ 1: not in O(3) versor group

geomstats_so3_in_GL3.detail: geomstats SO(3) identity belongs: O(3) ⊂ GL(3,R) confirmed via manifold

rustworkx_GL3_O3_edge_exists.detail: rustworkx: GL(3)→O(3) directed edge exists (first ratchet step)

rustworkx_GL3_O3_distance_1.detail: GL(3)→O(3) is a single reduction step (distance = 1)

GL3_minus_O3_exists.detail: Scaling matrix diag(3,1,1) ∈ GL(3,R)\O(3): det=3, M^TM ≠ I

O3_inclusion_in_GL3.detail: Identity ∈ O(3) ∩ GL(3,R): boundary where both shells coincide

gram_schmidt_of_O3_is_identity.detail: Gram-Schmidt of O(3) element stays in O(3): projection is idempotent on O(3)
